registry.description: The registry this snapshot targets — must equal the registry's id (registry SPEC §9: the host pins each prefix to one registry, so a snapshot is single-registry). A mismatch is a configuration error, refused typed.

modules.description: The enabled remotes to resolve. May be empty (nothing enabled ⇒ empty fragment).

shared.description: Shared-dependency majors the shell offers, keyed by bare specifier.

GateSnapshot.description: A gate snapshot: the request body of `POST /v1/resolve`. The enabled modules the host wants URLs for, plus what the shell offers for each shared specifier so resolution can scope different majors. `shared` is optional — a fragment of fully self-contained widgets needs none.

publisher.description: Publisher namespace prefix (unique within this registry, registry SPEC §9).

version.description: The exact SemVer of the enabled artifact.

GateModule.description: One enabled remote in a host's gate snapshot: an exact, source-qualified `(publisher, tag, version)` the host has decided to load. Versions are **exact** — this cut resolves a pinned version, never a version *set* or range (SCOPE cut: no version sets, GW-D19); the host's gate service already chose the version.

major.description: The SemVer major the shell offers at  {@link  url } .

url.description: The module URL the shell serves this major from (host-owned).

SharedOffer.description: One shared-dependency major the host **shell offers** for a bare specifier: the module URL the shell already provides for major `major`. A widget's manifest `sharedScope` declares the *range* it needs (GW-D22); resolution matches that range against these offers and emits a `scopes` entry when a widget needs a non-default major. The shell owns these URLs — the registry never hosts a shared dependency, it only scopes to what the shell declares ("never globals").
